Cosmo I — Neos Kosmos Residences Athens · Greece

The positioning

Between the city and the sea

Neos Kosmos holds a precise position in Athens. The historic centre — Syntagma, the Acropolis, the ancient agora — lies 2.9 kilometres north. The Athenian Riviera coastline extends south, reachable by tram from the building entrance. Three metro stations serve the wider neighbourhood. This dual axis is what defines the investment thesis: you are not choosing between the city and the coast. You have both.

Historic centre
Syntagma Square and the Acropolis equidistant at 2.9 km. Thirteen minutes by metro, fifteen on foot.

Riviera connection
Tram corridor links Neos Kosmos to Faliro, Alimos, and the Athenian Riviera coastline — accessible without a car.

The district

Neos Kosmos — an address shaped by daily life

7–10%
Annual price
appreciation · Athens
270m
Nearest metro
station
3
Metro stations
within 1.1km
10
Units in building —
limited supply

Neos Kosmos is not a tourist district. It developed as a residential area for permanent Athens residents, and that character has held. Streets are active with local commerce — cafés, bakeries, services, pharmacies 240 metres from the entrance. The absence of short-term tourism pressure means vacancy tracks structural demand: working residents, university-adjacent long-term tenants, diplomatic staff. That pattern produces the kind of occupancy that does not depend on a season.

Athens residential property has returned 7–10% annual price appreciation in recent years. That figure is driven by the same forces that make Neos Kosmos a durable address: insufficient residential supply, strong internal migration into Athens, and foreign buyer demand that has not yet reached the price levels seen in coastal and island markets. The entry point here is a fraction of what comparable access to city infrastructure costs in western Europe.

Expected yield and income are based on 3% gross return on purchase price. Not guaranteed by the developer. Income scales linearly with unit price — a €300,000 unit generates ~€9,000 / year gross.

Athens residential property values have shown 7–10% annual appreciation in recent years. Capital growth is separate from rental yield and is not guaranteed. Entry at €250,000 positions this investment well below the price levels at which comparable metro-adjacent assets trade in Western Europe.

Rooftop unit
Unit Δ1 — top floor

37 m² internal · 30.90 m² private rooftop terrace · total 67.90 m² · 1-bed / 1-bath. The only unit in the building with a private outdoor terrace of this scale.

Floors 1–3 (A–Γ)

Six apartments across three identical floors. Each floor has two layouts: 35.60 m² with 1 m² balcony, and 31 m² with 2.90 m² semi-balcony.

Ground floor (I1–I3)

Three ground-floor units including I1, a two-storey duplex at 53.17 m² with semi-balcony. I2 and I3 have private garden access.

All units

Turn-key delivery. Energy class A. Optional furnished packages available per unit type. No parking on ground floor units; upper floors by arrangement.

Zone temperature control per unit. Cooling SEER > 6.1. Aluminium frames with thermal break, U < 2.2 W/m²K.

Epirus Assets Cosmo I · Neos Kosmos · Athens

Golden Visa

EU residency through property — from €250,000

01
Select and reserve a unit
Choose from 10 units starting at €250,000. Cosmo I qualifies under the current Athens Zone B threshold. Verify classification at time of purchase as threshold boundaries are subject to revision.
02
Obtain Greek tax number
Greek TIN (AFM) is required before property purchase. Can be obtained remotely via a power of attorney. Standard process, typically one to two weeks.
03
Complete the purchase
Property transfer tax applies (approximately 3.09% of assessed value). Notarial fees and legal costs are additional. A Greek property lawyer handles due diligence and title.
04
Apply for residency
Five-year renewable residency permit. Includes principal applicant, spouse, and dependent children. No minimum stay requirement. Pathway to permanent residency after five years.

Cosmo I units from €250,000 qualify under the current Athens Zone B threshold — 68% below the €800,000 level applied to Athens Zone A and the most sought-after island markets. EU residency and a centrally located income-generating asset, at the minimum qualifying entry price.
